mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the unemployment rate below 7.5% signals stronger labor market conditions, boosting consumer spending and corporate earnings, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Unemployment rate declines below 7.5% but 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of cases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in residential construction (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicates strong consumer demand, which drives corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST and RSAFS both rise but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 40% of cases, or pairwise correlation between proxies and returns drops below 0.15.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 100 signals stronger manufacturing activity, which boosts corporate profits and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO rises > 0.5% MoM but 20-day forward returns are negative in > 30% of cases, or IC < 0.05 in 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI inflation reading above 2.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL exceeds 2.5% but 20-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of cases, or IC > 0.05 in 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) below 100k indicates weak labor demand, reducing corporate revenue visibility and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS < 100k but 5-day forward returns are positive in > 40% of cases, or Rank IC > 0.03 in 1-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth rate (GDPC1) above 3% signals robust economic expansion, increasing corporate earnings visibility and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 > 3% but 60-day forward returns are negative in > 35% of cases, or IC < 0.04 in 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 98.5 signals manufacturing weakness, reducing corporate margins and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO < 98.5 but 20-day forward returns are positive in > 40% of cases, or IC > 0.05 in 2-year rolling window.
